macro_segments.title: The Foundational Positions of Rav Chisda and Rami bar Chama on Stolen Objects Consumed by Children

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishna: Gozel u-Ma'akhil et Banav (Stealer Who Feeds His Children)
Rav Chisda's Position: Obligation to Pay Only After Ye'ush (Despair of Owner)
Rami bar Chama's Position: Reshut Yoresh ke-Reshut Lokeach (Heir's Possession Like Purchaser's)
Reconciling the Two Positions with the Mishna's Cases

macro_segments.title: The Braita of Rabbi Oshaya and Testing the Two Positions Against It

micro_segments.title: The Braita Teaching: When Gezeilah Is Kayemet (Extant), Return It; When Consumed, Exempt
Testing Against Rav Chisda: The Braita Implies Exemption Before Ye'ush
Rav Chisda's Response: The Braita Speaks Only After Ye'ush
Testing Against Rami bar Chama: The Braita Contradicts Reshut Yoresh ke-Reshut Lokeach
Rami bar Chama's Response: The Braita Speaks Before Ye'ush

macro_segments.title: Rava's Analysis: The Interest Money Case and the Limits of Reshut Yoresh ke-Reshut Lokeach

micro_segments.title: The Case of Interest Money Left to Children: Two Interpretations of Rami bar Chama
Rava's Position: Reshut Yoresh Is Not Like Reshut Lokeach, But the Obligation Is Personal
The Pasuk for Returning Interest: Vchi Achicha Imach and Its Limited Application
The Obligation to Return Interest Is Only on the One Who Charged It, Not the Heirs

macro_segments.title: Determining Whether Rami bar Chama's Teaching Applies to the Mishna or the Braita

micro_segments.title: If Applied to the Braita, the Principle Would Certainly Apply to the Mishna
If Applied to the Mishna, the Braita May Operate on Different Grounds (Personal Obligation)

macro_segments.title: The Mishna: First Case—Feeding the Children; Second Case—Leaving Before Them

micro_segments.title: First Part: Gozel u-Ma'akhil et Banav (Children Are Exempt from Payment)
Second Part: Hiniach Lifneihem with Gezeilah Be'ein (Adults Obligated, Minors Exempt)

macro_segments.title: When Gezeilah Is Be'ein (Extant): Adults Obligated, Minors Exempt, Unless Adults Claim Ignorance

micro_segments.title: Adult Children Claiming Ignorance of Father's Arrangement May Be Exempt

macro_segments.title: The Mishna: First Case—Feeding the Children; Second Case—Leaving Before Them (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rava's Reinterpretation: Adults Who Claim Knowledge of Father's Deal Are Exempt

macro_segments.title: Alternative Braita: Consumption of Stolen Object and the Question of Minor Liability

micro_segments.title: Alternative Braita Teaching: If Consumed, Both Adults and Minors Obligated
Problem: How Can Minors Be Obligated When There Is No Liability for Their Damage?
Rav Papa's Solution: Read as Still Around (Lo Achlu'um), Not Consumed

macro_segments.title: The Case of the Borrowed Cow: Obligations of the Borrower and Heir Versus Accident

micro_segments.title: Setup: Father Borrows Cow, Dies, Children Inherit the Right to Use It
If Cow Dies Naturally: Children Not Obligated in Onsin (Accident Liability)
If Children Unknowingly Slaughter and Eat: They Pay Two-Thirds Value (Benefit, Not Damage)
Relation to Achrayut Nechasim: Whether Real Estate Creates Liability for Minors

macro_segments.title: Rav Papa and the Timing of Obligation for a Shoal (Borrower): From Moment of Borrowing or From Damage

micro_segments.title: Rav Papa's Principle: Obligation of a Shoal Arises Only When Damage Occurs
Case: Cow Borrowed Thursday, Slaughtered Shabbat—No Payment Required
Contrasting View: Obligation Begins at Moment of Borrowing, Continues After Death
Application to Mishna: Whether Achrayut Applies to Cow Case Depends on When Obligation Begins

macro_segments.title: Pasuk-Based Teaching: Returning the Gezeilah and Its Application to Children Only When Not Consumed

micro_segments.title: Pasuk: Veheishiv et Ha-Gezeilah—Yachzir K'Ein She-Gazal (Return As It Was Stolen)
Application: Children Exempt When Gezeilah Is Consumed, Even If Father Consumed It
Justification: The Pasuk Teaches That Obligation to Return Applies Only When Object Exists

macro_segments.title: When Gezeilah Is Be'ein (Extant): Adults Obligated, Minors Exempt, Unless Adults Claim Ignorance (continued)

micro_segments.title: Adult Children Must Return Stolen Object If Still Extant
Minor Children Cannot Be Sued: The Principle of Mekablim Eidut She-Lo Bifnei Ba'al Din
Sumchos Position: Minors Are Exempt; Mainstream View: Minors Are Obligated

macro_segments.title: Case Law: Rabbi Yirmiya, Minors, and When Courts Adjudicate Against Minors

micro_segments.title: Scenario: Rabbi Yirmiya Claims Father Sold Property; Brother-in-Law (Heir, Minor) Refuses Entry
Rabbi Avin Refuses to Allow Suit: Cannot Bring Witnesses Against Status Quo Held by Minor
Counterargument from Braita: We Do Retrieve Stolen Objects from Minors
Distinction Introduced: Child Who Seized Another's Field vs. Child Holding Inherited Possession
The Status Quo Principle: We Preserve the Status Quo; Party Seeking Change Waits Until Minor Grows Up

macro_segments.title: Accepting Testimony Not in Presence of Defendant: Rules and Exceptions

micro_segments.title: General Rule: Mekablim Eidim She-Lo Bifnei Ba'al Din (Sometimes Yes, With Limitations)
Rabbi Yosi b'Rabbi Chanina: Exception Only When Urgent (Illness, Travel of Witnesses)
Additional Condition: Summons Sent and Defendant Refused to Appear
Rav Yehuda/Shmuel: If Court Case Already Opened, Defendant's Non-Appearance Allows Continuation
Right to Demand Major Beit Din: If Case Not Yet Opened, Defendant May Refuse Local Court

macro_segments.title: Validation of Documents (Kiyum Shtarot): When Witnesses Need Not Be Present

micro_segments.title: Rav's Position: Kiyum Shtar Can Be Done Without Defendant's Presence
Rabbi Yochanan's Objection: Pasuk Requires Defendant's Right to Face Witnesses
Rava's Resolution: Kiyum Shtar Is Rabbinic and Document Is Self-Authenticating

topical_tags.term: Reshut Yoresh ke-Reshut Lokeach (Heir's Possession Like Purchaser's)
Gozel u-Ma'akhil et Banav (Stealer Who Feeds Children)
Pasuk Vchi Achicha Imach (Your Brother Should Live With You)
Mekablim Eidut She-Lo Bifnei Ba'al Din (Accepting Testimony Without Defendant)
Qim Lei Be-Drabba Minei (Principle: Greater Obligation Overrides Lesser)
Hamotzi Me-Chaveiro Alav Ha-Rayah (Burden of Proof on Claimant)
